name: It's a Wonderful Life
description: Frank Capra's 1946 Christmas classic — George Bailey, Bedford Falls, and Clarence the angel.

questions.q: Who directed 'It's a Wonderful Life'?

source.notes: Capra's first film after returning from his WWII service.

questions.q: Who plays the lead role of George Bailey?

source.notes: It was Stewart's first film after returning from WWII service as a bomber pilot.

questions.q: Who plays Mary Hatch, George Bailey's wife?
Who plays the villainous banker Mr. Potter?
What is the name of George Bailey's hometown in the film?

source.notes: Fictional town often associated with Seneca Falls, NY, which inspired its look.

questions.q: In the alternate reality where George was never born, what is Bedford Falls renamed?
What is the name of George Bailey's guardian angel?

source.notes: An 'Angel Second Class' working to earn his wings. Played by Henry Travers.

questions.q: What kind of business does George Bailey run?
How much money does Uncle Billy misplace, triggering the film's crisis?

source.notes: Roughly equivalent to $130,000 in today's dollars.

questions.q: What is the famous closing line of the film, said by Zuzu about angels?
choices: Every time a bell rings, an angel gets his wings.
Each chime brings a soul home.

source.original_a: "Every time a bell rings, an angel gets his wings"

questions.q: What is the name of George Bailey's youngest daughter, who delivers the closing line?

source.notes: The flower petals 'Zuzu's petals' become George's tangible proof he's back in the real world.

questions.q: What is the name of George Bailey's brother, who becomes a WWII hero?

source.notes: Harry Bailey wins the Medal of Honor for stopping a kamikaze attack on a troopship.

questions.q: In what year was 'It's a Wonderful Life' released?
What 1939 short story is the film based on?

source.notes: Written by Philip Van Doren Stern; he sent it as a Christmas card after publishers passed.

questions.q: Which song is sung at the end of the film?
Which studio distributed 'It's a Wonderful Life' on its original 1946 release?
What technical Oscar achievement is the film credited with introducing?
choices: A new method of simulating falling snow

source.original_a: For the development of a new method of simulating falling snow
source.notes: RKO's compound of water, soap flakes, foamite, and sugar — replacing the previous standard of painted cornflakes.

questions.q: What was the working title of the screenplay before being renamed?

source.notes: Same as the original short story title.

questions.q: What Christmas-Eve-set act does George contemplate before Clarence intervenes?

source.notes: George considers jumping from a bridge; Clarence jumps first to save him.

questions.q: Who played Uncle Billy, the absent-minded relative who misplaces the deposit?

source.notes: Mitchell also played Scarlett's father in 'Gone with the Wind' and Doc Boone in 'Stagecoach' (both 1939).
